questions.q: What is the purpose of tabulation?
questions.answer: Tabulation is the process of presenting data in a systematic and organized manner using tables. It helps in easy identification of trends, patterns, and relationships between different variables. Tabulation also facilitates comparison and analysis of data.

questions.q: What is the difference between ungrouped and grouped frequency distribution?
questions.answer: Ungrouped frequency distribution is a table that shows the actual values of a variable, whereas grouped frequency distribution is a table that shows the values of a variable grouped into class intervals. Grouped frequency distribution helps in identifying patterns and trends in data.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a histogram?
questions.answer: A histogram is a graphical representation of a continuous frequency distribution that shows the shape of the distribution and the frequency of different values. It helps in identifying the mode, median, and skewness of the distribution.

questions.q: What is the difference between a bar chart and a histogram?
questions.answer: A bar chart is a graphical representation of categorical data, whereas a histogram is a graphical representation of continuous data. A bar chart shows the frequency or value of different categories, whereas a histogram shows the frequency of different values in a continuous distribution.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a pie chart?
questions.answer: A pie chart is a circular graphical representation of categorical data that shows the proportion or percentage of different categories. It helps in identifying the relative importance of different categories.

questions.q: What is the difference between a frequency polygon and a histogram?
questions.answer: A frequency polygon is a graphical representation of a frequency distribution that shows the shape of the distribution using a line, whereas a histogram is a graphical representation of a frequency distribution that shows the shape of the distribution using bars. A frequency polygon is more accurate than a histogram.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a scatter diagram?
questions.answer: A scatter diagram is a graphical representation of two variables that shows the relationship between them. It helps in identifying the type of relationship (positive, negative, or no relationship) between the variables.

questions.q: What is the difference between a time series graph and a scatter diagram?
questions.answer: A time series graph is a graphical representation of data that shows the trend and pattern over a period of time, whereas a scatter diagram is a graphical representation of two variables that shows the relationship between them. A time series graph shows the trend and pattern, whereas a scatter diagram shows the relationship between variables.

questions.q: What is the purpose of a Lorenz curve?
questions.answer: A Lorenz curve is a graphical representation of income distribution that shows the relationship between poverty and inequality. It helps in identifying the level of poverty and inequality in a society.

questions.q: What is the difference between a simple bar chart and a component bar chart?
questions.answer: A simple bar chart is a graphical representation of categorical data that shows the frequency or value of different categories, whereas a component bar chart is a graphical representation of categorical data that shows the contribution of different components to the total. A simple bar chart shows the total, whereas a component bar chart shows the contribution of different components.
